titleStmt.title: Saudi Arabia 2022 – Speaker 20 – Shepherd
WIBARAB Corpus
titleStmt.funder: Funded by the European Research Council under the Grant Agreement 101020127

publicationStmt.distributor: Austrian Center for Digital Humanities and Cultural Heritage

recording.p: Recorded during Saudi Arabia Fieldwork Campaign Gunda Kinzl 2022

prefixDef.p: Private URIs using the teiCorpusHeader prefix are pointers to any element in the WIBARAB teiCorpus document.
Private URIs using the share prefix are pointers to audio file residing on the WIBARAB network share.
Private URIs using the geo prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on a place element in VICAV Geodata list.
Private URIs using the dmp prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on an element in the WIBARAB DMP document..
Private URIs using the zotid prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on a biblStruct element in the TEI export of the VICAV Zotero Group library.
Private URIs using the sources prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on an element in the WIBARAB list of sources.

spanGrp.span: The goat's kid (bidil baḥam), the young child of a goat (that)
I used to roam here when I was (the father of) eight years old
eight years old, I was young
After that, I grew up until I reached eleven
I became a shepherd of sheep, sheep
(I became) a bit older, I roamed within it in the spring, in the desert, I came
After fourteen, fifteen years I became (a shepherd) of camels
of the camels, meaning I roamed in the night, I would bring this there, and this camel here and I would come and sleep here
Next to, next to the she-camel, the she-camel that came from the camel
The she-camel, we call the female she-camel
from the camel, we sleep at his, I sleep here
The offspring(?) Even when they see the fox, they protect me from it
She wakes me up, she wakes me up meaning she moves, you know?
I stayed until probably fifteen years with camels
Afterwards, I stayed around three years
I left pasturing camels. I took two months, three months
We live on one date and on the camel s milk
The camels take about a month and it would not come back to you
I am not worried about him like I am for the sheep
The camels, the fox doesn't come to them, it goes to the younger camels.
The small camel, the child of the she-camel
Or the she-camel, we call her the she-camel, the camels
And so, we are not afraid for them like we are with the sheep
The sheep, naturally you worry about them, you worry that the fox would eat them, the camels, no, it doesnt come to them
The she-camel releases her child, she releases it
It starts later to look into the eyes of the fox and it shakes and strikes the fox.
so, I lived a life as a shepherd since I was a child
The sheep, and the goat's kid and the sheep and the camels, and this is our life in the desert
the last two months
and I was young, from the age of fifteen
I would roam with the camels, I had a date, the camels's milk, a little # and I would put that over the fire
We brought, with me the small bowl and we put the flour like this
I light the fire until its cooked, I bring the camels milk and I put this wheat until its cooked. and we eat it
and my health was good, nothing missing
My health was good, until I reached sixteen
after I turned sixteen I started to get educated
I was learning at the late hours, meaning I would go to learn in the evening, in the evening, I only studied in the evening
he considers it a reward (the education)
No, in the morning no, we were older children, the morning was for the younger children, eight year olds
they were six years and upwards, we were older, sixteen years old
meaning, we studied by night, the education, I studied
Meaning, I studied writing and reading since I got my education I got my ID
which is the 'belonging ID', the 'belonging ID'
which is the ID, nowadays, we call it the ID
but before we used to call it the 'belonging ID'
the ID
I received my ID and I went and worked
and my education continued up to the secondary level
Afterwards, thank God, I learned, I studied life, we got maried, we built a family, we built a house
and thank God, life was good
and this is the Bedouin life
It didn't keep us from learning, it didn't keep us from anything that we wanted, we didn't know anything before and we didn't know how to read or write.
however, I dont think that it was a barrier between me and education
We learned, we studied life, we experienced life and all of it was a wonder from its beginning to now, it was really nice, nice
We, the bedouins love now, I love the camels a lot
I love the camels a lot
all of them which we have, they all love the camels, thank you.
